name: discord-qdrant-version-upgrade-upgrade-result-drift
prompt: We recently upgraded our Qdrant server from v1.15.5 to v1.18.0 and noticed significant differences in search results for the same queries against the same database. Setup: Collection uses scalar quantization, embedding model ibm-granite/granite-embedding-107m-multilingual, database unchanged between both versions. Observed issues: (1) Score mismatch - same top result in both versions, but different similarity score. (2) Result mismatch - a different query returns a completely different top result depending on version. Could you help us understand if there were any changes in v1.16-v1.18 related to scalar quantization scoring or ranking that could explain this behavior? [CSV of before/after scores per query attached] We didn't touch anything, devops just updated the cluster from 1.15.5 to 1.18.0. No quantile set. Cosine distance.

rubric.text: States that nothing is wrong with the data or the upgrade itself
Explains that starting in v1.16.3, query quantization changed from truncation to rounding when converting vectors to int8
Notes that truncation introduced a small downward bias in scores, and rounding removes that bias
Explains that since queries are quantized on every search, score changes can appear immediately after upgrade even with no re-ingestion
Notes the effect is small and mostly preserves ranking, though very close results can swap positions
Recommends enabling rescoring (with oversampling) to get stable scores across versions, since rescoring recomputes scores from the original float vectors independent of int8 quantization
Asks clarifying questions (whether segments were rebuilt, whether a quantile is set, which distance metric) before giving a definitive explanation
Attributes the score/result differences to data corruption, embedding model drift, or a bug requiring re-ingestion or rollback, instead of the documented quantization rounding change
